commands.review_summary:
Adds `rfcs/0010-feed-observability-and-telemetry.md`, a draft RFC addendum covering feed diagnostics, OpenTelemetry mapping, install-record provenance, privacy boundaries, and rollout questions.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR adds an RFC design document, not a current behavior bug. The concrete checks are the proposed Markdown file, README lifecycle rules, and open RFC numbering state.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers need to decide RFC acceptance, telemetry/privacy direction, and numbering; there is no safe automated repair to queue.

target.title: RFC: Feed observability and usage telemetry addendum
target.body:
## Summary

Follow-up addendum to RFC 0009 covering feed observability and usage telemetry.

This clarifies that OpenClaw can reuse the existing diagnostics and OpenTelemetry pipeline, but feed measurement will not happen automatically unless clients preserve feed provenance from fetch through install and activation.

## What this adds

- Defines feed lifecycle diagnostic events for fetch, snapshot load, entry action, update, policy decision, and activation.
- Explains the current measurement model: diagnostics events, local diagnostics bundles, diagnostics-otel, and installed plugin records already exist, but they do not currently preserve feed id/revision attribution.
- Proposes a small provenance block in feed-backed install records so later plugin or skill activation can be attributed to the accepted feed snapshot.
- Sets privacy boundaries: no prompts, tool payloads, skill contents, secrets, raw URLs, local paths, raw user ids, or raw tenant ids.
- Lists the admin and publisher reports this should enable, including adoption, stale clients, fallback rate, blocked demand, install/update failure rate, and revocation propagation.

## Validation

- `git diff --check`
- checked no em dashes in the new RFC
- checked no non-ASCII characters in the new RFC
